description: The Straight Satans Motorcycle Club was a one-percenter outlaw motorcycle gang based in Southern California, founded in July 1959 by three bikers known as Father Bill, Little Jerry, and Glick. It was among the earliest outlaw motorcycle clubs in the Los Angeles area, but remained localized without chapters beyond its original base. The club gained notoriety for its association with the Manson Family during the late 1960s, particularly around the Tate–LaBianca murders. It was implicated in criminal incidents including a sexual assault in 1972.
